title: Fix stale reference false positives: capitalized English words misidentified as code symbols
description: The drift detector treats capitalized English words in markdown prose (e.g. Backward, Breaking, Communication) as Go symbols and flags them as stale when not found in code. This causes 60-70% false positive rate on stale references. Root cause is in the markdown-to-symbol extraction logic. Fix: only match words that appear in backticks, follow code-reference patterns (pkg.Symbol, camelCase, snake_case), or are confirmed against the actual symbol index. Discovered during x40.11 validation across 16 OSS repos.
notes:
PLAN: Root cause is goIdentRe in drift.go line 63 matching ANY PascalCase word from prose, filtered only by a static commonWords map (which can never be complete). Fix: change prose extraction to only match words with code-like patterns (contains dot, mixed case after first char like camelCase, contains underscore, or has 2+ internal uppercase). Backtick-quoted identifiers already handled correctly. Will add isCodeLikeIdentifier() function and update the prose extraction block.
PROGRESS: Replaced goIdentRe + commonWords blocklist approach with proseCodeRefRe that uses structural pattern matching. Prose words are now only treated as symbols if they contain dots (pkg.Symbol), camelCase, snake_case, or PascalCase with multiple uppercase transitions (ConfigMap, NewInformerFunc). Backtick extraction unchanged. All 18 tests pass, zero regressions. K8s validation shows significant false positive reduction.
close_reason: DONE: Replaced blocklist-based prose symbol extraction (goIdentRe + 130-entry commonWords map) with structural pattern matching (proseCodeRefRe). Prose words are now only treated as code symbols if they match code-like patterns: dotted refs (pkg.Symbol), camelCase, snake_case, or PascalCase with multiple uppercase transitions (ConfigMap). Plain capitalized English words like Backward, Breaking, Communication are no longer false positives. Backtick extraction unchanged. 4 new test cases added, all 18 tests pass with zero regressions.

notes:

For each repo: (1) record total findings, (2) manually classify each as true positive or false positive, (3) file a GitHub issue on the repo with the drift report, (4) track if maintainer responds/engages. Decision gate: <3/10 maintainer engagement = revisit product strategy.
SCOPE EXPANSION: Added adsabs GitHub org (Python-heavy astrophysics repos — tests cross-language support beyond Go) and sourcegraph/sourcegraph monorepo (large Go+TS codebase with heavy documentation — stress test for scale). These complement the original 10 Go-focused repos by validating livedocs works across languages and repo sizes.
PLAN: Build livedocs, shallow-clone repos into /tmp/livedocs-validation/, run 'livedocs check' on each, capture timing and structured JSON output. Three groups: (1) 10 Go repos, (2) 3-5 adsabs repos with most markdown, (3) sourcegraph monorepo.
PROGRESS: Completed validation against all 16 repos (10 Go, 5 adsabs, 1 sourcegraph monorepo). All repos cloned --depth=1. livedocs check run on every repo in both text and JSON format. Results collected: timing, stale refs, undocumented exports, false positive analysis.
RESULTS SUMMARY:

| bumblebee        | 10       | 139        | 0            | 24         | 16        |

== KEY METRICS ==
- Time to first finding: <10ms on all repos (sub-second for every repo)
- Largest repo (grafana, 994 md files): 598ms
- Sourcegraph monorepo (917 md files): 642ms

== FALSE POSITIVE ANALYSIS ==
Stale refs false positive rate (heuristic: English words treated as symbols):
- cobra: 70% FP rate (548/778 are English words like 'Backward', 'Breaking', 'Cobra')
- prometheus: 61% FP rate (2779/4532 are English words)

Undocumented exports quality: MUCH better — these are real Go exported symbols (e.g. Configs.MarshalYAML, NewManager, StaticConfig) not mentioned in READMEs. True positive rate near 100%.

adsabs repos: 0 undocumented exports found because livedocs only supports Go code extraction, not Python.

== SIGNAL-TO-NOISE RATIO ==
- Stale references: LOW signal (60-70% false positives). Root cause: symbol extractor treats capitalized English words in markdown as code references.
- Undocumented exports: HIGH signal (near 100% true positive). Genuinely exported Go symbols missing from docs.
- Stale package refs: MODERATE signal. Some false positives from path-like strings (e.g. 'and/or', 'key/value').
- Cross-language: No Python/TS symbol extraction yet — adsabs repos only produce stale ref noise.

== CRITICAL FINDING ==
The stale reference detector has a fundamental false positive problem: it extracts capitalized words from markdown prose and flags them as 'code symbols not found in code.' This makes the stale ref count essentially meaningless for repos with narrative documentation (CHANGELOGs, CONTRIBUTINGs, etc.). The undocumented export detector is solid.
close_reason: DONE: Validated livedocs check against 16 repos (10 Go, 5 adsabs/Python, 1 sourcegraph monorepo). Performance excellent: sub-second for all repos including 994-md-file grafana (598ms) and sourcegraph monorepo (642ms). Undocumented export detection is high-quality (near 100% TP rate for Go repos). CRITICAL ISSUE: stale reference detection has 60-70% false positive rate — extracts capitalized English words from markdown prose as 'symbols.' Cross-language support absent (Python/TS repos produce 0 undocumented findings). Decision gate: product is NOT ready for external outreach until stale ref FP rate is fixed.

title: Profile go/packages memory on 500-package k8s subset
description: Run go/packages with NeedTypes|NeedDeps on 500 packages from kubernetes/kubernetes. Measure RSS peak. If >8GB, implement topological-layer extraction with intermediate disk writes. Must happen in week 1, not month 3. Premortem theme 3: 3/5 lenses predicted 14GB+ RSS explosion.
notes:
PLAN: (1) Initialize Go module in live_docs. (2) Write cmd/memprofile/main.go that uses go/packages to load k8s packages with NeedTypes|NeedDeps|NeedSyntax. (3) Monitor RSS via /proc/self/status at checkpoints of 50, 100, 200, 500 packages. (4) Run against ~/kubernetes/kubernetes/. (5) If RSS >8GB at 500 packages, design topological-layer extraction with intermediate disk writes. (6) Capture results in validation/memory_profile_results.md.
PROGRESS: Profiling complete. RSS peak 3.5GB at 1372 packages (full k8s monorepo). Well under 8GB budget. go/packages shares transitive deps — cost is dominated by the shared dependency graph (~2.8GB), not per-package cost. No topological-layer extraction needed. Error rate is a Go version mismatch (k8s requires go1.26, system has go1.25.7) but types/syntax/deps still load successfully.
close_reason: DONE: RSS peak was 3.5GB at 1372 packages (full k8s monorepo). Well under 8GB budget. go/packages shares transitive dependency graph (~2.8GB base cost) with ~0.5MB marginal per-package. Scaling is sub-linear: 50->1372 pkgs = 2.8->3.5GB RSS. Types/syntax/deps loaded successfully for 99%+ packages. Load time: 1.5-2.2s per checkpoint. DECISION: Topological-layer extraction NOT needed. The Go deep extractor (acp.2) can safely load all packages in a single go/packages.Load call. Premortem 14GB prediction was overly pessimistic by 4x.

title: Tribal facts: symbol-level linking for MCP tools
description: Tribal extractors currently create file-level subjects (file paths as symbol names) rather than linking to existing per-symbol entries in the symbols table. This means MCP tools like tribal_owners('ClaimsDB') return nothing because facts are keyed to 'db/claims.go' not 'ClaimsDB'. Fix: (1) MCP tribal tools should accept file paths and resolve them to all symbols in that file, OR (2) extractors should link facts to individual symbols when possible (blame has line ranges, inline markers have line numbers that can be matched to symbol ranges). title: Evaluate wazero WASM runtime for tree-sitter (replace CGO)
description: Prototype tree-sitter-go via wazero WASM runtime. Measure: parse speed (must be <500ms/file), memory overhead, grammar version pinning. Compare against CGO bindings. Eliminates CGO build fragility and cross-platform issues. Premortem theme 2: CGO dependency identified as fragile by 2/5 lenses.
notes:
PLAN: Evaluate 3 tree-sitter Go approaches: (1) smacker/go-tree-sitter (CGO baseline), (2) malivvan/tree-sitter (wazero-based, exactly matching PRD spec), (3) odvcencio/gotreesitter (pure Go, no CGO, 436 stars, 206 grammars). Benchmark on 5 k8s Go files (52-8529 lines). Measure: parse time, memory alloc, correctness (AST node count + function extraction). Verdict criteria: wazero must parse <500ms/file per PRD.
RESULTS: CGO (smacker/go-tree-sitter) is 12-7500x faster than pure Go (odvcencio/gotreesitter). CGO: 85us-8ms range. PureGo: 1.7ms-40s range. types.go (8529 lines) takes 40s in pure Go vs 5ms in CGO. kubelet.go (3579 lines) borderline at 500ms. Correctness: function extraction matches 100%. malivvan/tree-sitter (wazero) only supports C/C++ grammars at v0.0.1 -- unusable for Go.
close_reason: DONE: Wazero WASM is NOT viable — the only Go library (malivvan/tree-sitter v0.0.1) lacks Go grammar support. Pure Go alternative (odvcencio/gotreesitter v0.12.2) is 12-7500x slower than CGO with 310MB+ memory on large files, failing the <500ms PRD requirement on files >3000 lines. CGO tree-sitter (smacker/go-tree-sitter) remains the only viable option at 85us-8ms parse times. Verdict: keep CGO with static linking + Docker build mitigation. Re-evaluate when wazero ecosystem matures.

title: ADR: Claims-backed generation with verification gates
description:
Decision: Store documentation as structured claims (tuples) in SQLite, not as prose files. Three-tier model: Tier 1 (AST-derived, auto-ship), Tier 2 (LLM semantic, verification gate), Tier 3 (human-written, drift-detect only).

Rationale: Emerged from structured debate between Detection-First, Generation-First, and Structured Claims DB positions. All three converged on claims as the integration layer. Detection provides trust infrastructure. Generation fills the 94% absence gap. Claims DB makes both composable.

Research: 5-agent divergent research + 3-advocate convergent debate. See convergence_report.md and prd_live_documentation_system.md.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (post-premortem revision): Claims-backed generation with verification gates adopted. Key premortem modifications: (1) stateless hook architecture, (2) usefulness validation gate before building, (3) Tier 1 redefined as 'enhanced go doc' not GoDoc duplicate, (4) Phase 0 added for output validation. See premortem_live_docs_architecture.md themes 3-5.

title: ADR: Use SCIP symbol format, not SCIP tooling
description:
Decision: Adopt SCIP's symbol string format as canonical ID but build primary extractor on Go stdlib (go/packages + go/types + go/doc). Use tree-sitter for incremental hot path. Optionally consume SCIP indexes for cross-repo resolution.

Rationale: scip-go wraps the same Go stdlib packages, adds 60% irrelevant data (syntax highlighting, diagnostics), breaks on every Go release, has no incremental indexing, and is pre-1.0. go/doc is purpose-built for documentation extraction. SCIP symbol format is valuable for cross-repo joins across 79 kubernetes repos.

Research: 5-agent divergent research (feasibility, data model, incremental, multi-repo, alternatives). See prd_scip_implementation.md.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (post-premortem revision): SCIP symbol format demoted from primary key to secondary index. Primary identity is composite repo+import_path+name. Rationale: 3/5 premortem lenses identified SCIP format as highest-confidence risk (staging replace variants, pre-1.0 spec drift, Go-specific encoding). WASM tree-sitter replaces CGO bindings. Per-repo SQLite replaces monolithic DB. See premortem_live_docs_architecture.md themes 1-2.

description: Parse index.scip files using github.com/sourcegraph/scip Go bindings. Populate claims DB from external SCIP indexes. Enables cross-repo symbol resolution across 79 kubernetes repos via SCIP symbol string join key.

title: Build content-hash caching layer
description: SHA-256 hash per source file stored in source_files table. On each commit, compute hashes for changed files only. Skip extraction for unchanged files. Turns O(n) into O(changed). Essential for making per-commit updates affordable.
notes:
PREMORTEM UPDATE: Cache key must include content_hash + extractor_version + grammar_version. Add LRU eviction at 2GB cap. Add deletion-aware reconciliation (tombstone on git rm). See premortem themes 2-3.
PLAN: Content-hash caching layer design.

## Cache Key
Composite: SHA256(file_content) + extractor_version + grammar_version
- Stored in source_files table per PRD schema
- Cache hit = all three match → skip extraction

## LRU Eviction (2GB cap)
- Track total cache size via per-entry byte count stored in source_files
- On insert, if total > 2GB, evict least-recently-used entries (by last_indexed timestamp)
- Eviction removes source_files row AND associated claims

## Deletion-Aware Reconciliation
- On each git diff, identify deleted/renamed files
- Mark deleted files with deleted=1 tombstone (soft delete)
- Tombstoned entries excluded from queries, eligible for eviction first
- Reconcile() scans git diff output, marks removals, inserts new files

## Core API
- CacheStore interface: Get/Put/Delete/Reconcile/Evict
- SQLite-backed implementation
- Get(repo, path, contentHash, extractorVer, grammarVer) → (cached bool)
- Put(repo, path, contentHash, extractorVer, grammarVer, sizeBytes)
- MarkDeleted(repo, path)
- Reconcile(repo, currentFiles map[path]hash, deletedPaths []string)
- Evict() → evict LRU until under 2GB cap

## Implementation Files
- /home/ds/live_docs/cache/cache.go — interface + types
- /home/ds/live_docs/cache/sqlite.go — SQLite implementation
- /home/ds/live_docs/cache/cache_test.go — tests
PROGRESS: Implementation complete. 21 tests pass, 76.4% coverage, race-free, go vet clean. Files: cache/cache.go (interface+types), cache/sqlite.go (SQLite impl), cache/cache_test.go (21 tests). Pure-Go SQLite via modernc.org/sqlite (no CGO).
close_reason: DONE: Content-hash caching layer implemented in Go under /home/ds/live_docs/cache/. Three files: cache.go (Store interface + Entry type), sqlite.go (SQLite-backed implementation with WAL mode, pure-Go via modernc.org/sqlite), cache_test.go (21 tests). Cache key is composite SHA-256(content) + extractor_version + grammar_version per PRD spec. LRU eviction at configurable cap (default 2GB) with tombstoned entries evicted first. Deletion-aware reconciliation: Reconcile() diffs current files against cache, tombstones missing files, returns changed/new paths for re-extraction. All tests pass with -race flag. 76.4% statement coverage (remaining gaps are defensive SQL error branches). go vet clean.

title: Implement SCIP-compatible symbol string format
description: Generate symbol IDs in SCIP format: <scheme> <manager> <package> <version> <descriptor>. Descriptor suffixes: / (namespace), # (type), . (term), (). (method). This enables cross-repo symbol resolution across 79 kubernetes repos without depending on scip-go tooling.
notes:
Reference: Symbol format details in prd_scip_implementation.md (Design Considerations > Why adopt SCIP's symbol format). Format: <scheme> <manager> <package> <version> <descriptor>. Descriptor suffixes: / (namespace), # (type), . (term), (). (method).
PREMORTEM UPDATE: SCIP symbol format demoted from primary key to nullable secondary index. Primary identity is composite key (repo+import_path+name). SCIP strings are generated optionally for external tooling compat. Scope reduced — this is no longer a blocking dependency for the Go extractor. Consider deprioritizing or folding into schema bead.
PLAN: Implement scip package under /home/ds/live_docs/scip/ with:
1. symbol.go — Symbol type + FormatSymbol() that produces SCIP-format strings: <scheme> ' ' <manager> ' ' <package> ' ' <version> ' ' <descriptor>+
2. Descriptor suffixes per SCIP spec: / (namespace), # (type), . (term), (method). (method with parens)
3. Go descriptor encoding: package→namespace/, type→type#, func→func(). method→method(). var/const→name. 
4. Escape rules: space→single-space delimiter between components, backtick-escaping for special chars in identifiers
5. ParseSymbol() to round-trip from string back to Symbol struct
6. symbol_test.go — Table-driven tests covering: Go symbols (pkg, type, func, method, const/var), edge cases (special chars, empty fields), round-trip parse/format
7. TDD: tests first, then implement, verify coverage >80%
PROGRESS: All tests pass (32/32), 88.9% coverage, go vet clean. Files: scip/symbol.go (implementation), scip/symbol_test.go (tests).
close_reason: DONE: Implemented SCIP-compatible symbol string format in scip/ package. Symbol type with Format()/ParseSymbol() round-trips the SCIP format: <scheme> <manager> <package> <version> <descriptor>+. Descriptor suffixes: / (namespace), # (type), . (term), (). (method). FormatGoSymbol() convenience function derives descriptors from Go module/package paths. Backtick escaping for special chars. 32 table-driven tests, 88.9% coverage, go vet clean.
